As requested. this letter serves to formally request your agency's advice
regarding C'overnment code Section 87200 as recently changeil by Chapter
403 .
Specifically my questions relate to the requirement that Form 721 be filecl

expanded the definition of 'rother public officialsrr to include
I'consultants who have decision making authority . 
.". I was informed. by
our County Counsel that a irconsultant" is further defined by the FPPC to
be a 'rnatural person who provides, under contract, information, advice.
recomnendations or counsel
The countyrE Retirement Board retains money managers to invest the assets
of the pension trust. In doing so, it specifies the amount of funds for
which the manager is to be responsible, the asset class in which the
manager may invest (stocks, bonds, tlomestic, global, etc,), the objective
rate of return expected, the benchmark index against which performance
wiII be measured, and any other limitation on the Manager's discretion,
The decision as to how rm:ch of its total portfolio should go into any one
asset class j.s solely the Board's. The Board reviercs managers'
performance guarterly at a minimun, lhe alecision to hire or fi-re a
manager is solely the Board's. Withj-n the parameters set forth in the
contract, managers are given sole dj-scretion as to the timing and
selection of securities trades. A1I trades are reported daily to the
Association office.
My review of the pertinent law, the r'PPc Anridelines gi,ven to date, and my
knolvledge of the contlactual arrangement between the Board and its money

managers, leads me to conclude that our money managers are not
"consultantstr:
- Our money managers are firms, not rrnatural persons,'.
- Theyrre retained, under contract, to buy and seII securities not to
provide information, advj-ce, reconmendations or counsel.
- They're retained for their investment style and, while their
performance may be indirectly affected, they are not judged on their
market timing or trade activj-ties.
- We evaluate money managers on their ability to meet or exceecl return
objectives while adhering to the j-nvestment style for which they were
retained.
- ALI proxy votes are cast by the Board.
- No assets, except for short term cash to facilitate trades, are held
by the money manager, but rather by the Associationrs custodian bank.
- No one individual at the money manager firm is solely responsible for
decisions related to our portion of their total portfolio (see
Attachment A).
- AII money managers (firms) are required to registe! thej.r activities
with the SEC on form ADV, "Unj.form Applicatj.on for Investment Advisor
Registration". Ehis form is extensive in its information
requirements (see Attachment B), Part II of this form is annually
provided to the Association by each mErnager and avaj.lable to the
general pu-blic.
FinaIIy, if it is determj-ned that our money manager firms are t'pu-blic
officialsrr as used in c,overnment Code Section 872OO, T request guidance on
who at the firm is to fiIe.
As is noted on Attachnent A, and if everyone enployed by the firm needs to
file, the FPPC could receive over 1000 forms from contra Costa managers
alone. If every public pension plan in California does liJ<ewise, this
figure can easily be rultiplied by 20 or 30, since each of us has from one
to ten (or more ) money managers and theyrre not the same finns.


Ms. Linda Moureaux
January 22, 7990
Page 3
If these firms are required to file, I believe the Fppc,s intent could be
easlly and efficiently served by obtaining coples of SEC Form ADV from the
money managers, from the pension plans or directly from the SEC.
I await your reply before contacting any money managers on this filing
reguirement .

The Board contracts with money managers who then follow either an active

This style of investment replicates a pre-selected index such as the S & p

we try to answer all advice requests pronptly.

sections.question: My review of the pertinent law, the FPPC Guidelines given to date, and my knowledge of the contractual arrangement between the Board and its money managers, leads me to conclude that our money managers are not 'consultants' and therefore not required to file Form 721 as 'other public officials who manage public investments.' Does the FPPC agree with this interpretation? Additionally, if the FPPC determines that money manager firms are 'public officials' under Government Code Section 87200, who specifically at the firm would be required to file?
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks whether money manager firms retained by Contra Costa County Employees' Retirement Association to invest pension assets are required to file Form 721 as 'other public officials' under Government Code Section 87200, and if so, which individuals at those firms would need to file.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC dated January 22, 1990. The FPPC's acknowledgment letter dated January 31, 1990 is included but contains no substantive response yet. The document includes detailed attachments explaining money manager firm structure and SEC Form ADV requirements. Question and conclusion are synthesized from the body of the request letter as no explicit Q/A sections are present.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: My review of the pertinent law, the FPPC Guidelines given to date, and my knowledge of the contractual arrangement between the Board and its money managers, leads me to conclude that our money managers are not 'consultants' and therefore not required to file Form 721 as 'other public officials who manage public investments.' Does the FPPC agree with this interpretation? Additionally, if the FPPC determines that money manager firms are 'public officials' under Government Code Section 87200, who specifically at the firm would be required to file?

embedding.summary: Contra Costa County Employees' Retirement Association requests FPPC advice on whether external money manager firms managing pension assets constitute 'other public officials' requiring Form 721 filing under Government Code Section 87200, arguing they should not be classified as consultants given their organizational structure, SEC registration, and limited decision-making authority within contractually specified parameters.
